segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why does Tazieff risk his life like this?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么塔齐耶夫要这样冒生命危险？

segments.text: Haroun Tazieff, the Polish scientist, has spent his lifetime studying active volcanoes and deep caves in all parts of the world.

translations.zh-Hans: 波兰科学家哈伦·塔齐耶夫毕生都在研究世界各地的活火山和深洞穴。

segments.text: In 1948, he went to Lake Kivu in the Congo to observe a new volcano which he later named Kituro.

translations.zh-Hans: 1948年，他前往刚果的基伍湖考察一座新火山，后来将其命名为基图罗火山。

segments.text: Tazieff was able to set up his camp very close to the volcano while it was erupting violently.

translations.zh-Hans: 火山猛烈喷发时，塔齐耶夫竟能把营地扎在离火山非常近的地方。

segments.text: Though he managed to take a number of brilliant photographs, he could not stay near the volcano for very long.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管他拍下了许多精彩照片，却无法在火山附近久留。

segments.text: He noticed that a river of liquid rock was coming towards him.

translations.zh-Hans: 他发现一股熔岩正向他涌来。

segments.text: It threatened to surround him completely, but Tazieff managed to escape just in time.

translations.zh-Hans: 熔岩眼看就要把他完全包围，塔齐耶夫却在最后一刻成功逃脱。

segments.text: He waited until the volcano became quiet and he was able to return two days later.

translations.zh-Hans: 他一直等到火山平静下来，两天后才得以返回。

segments.text: This time, he managed to climb into the mouth of Kituro so that he could take photographs and measure temperatures.

translations.zh-Hans: 这一次，他设法爬进基图罗火山口，以便拍照并测量温度。

segments.text: Tazieff has often risked his life in this way.

translations.zh-Hans: 塔齐耶夫经常以这种方式冒生命危险。

segments.text: He has been able to tell us more about active volcanoes than any man alive.

translations.zh-Hans: 他让我们对活火山的了解超过了当世任何其他人。
